description: Evaluation set for snappi-script-generator-agent: OTG config to executable Snappi script

eval_questions.question: Generate a Snappi script from otg_config.json (BGP convergence test, 2 ports, 1000 pps, 30s timeout). Infrastructure: controller at http://localhost:8443, ports at location_1:5555 and location_2:5556.
expected_outputs: Valid Python file (test_bgp_convergence.py or similar)
Script includes: imports, config creation, connection, BGP setup, traffic, assertions
Controller URL and port locations injected
Proper error handling (try/except/finally)
Executable as-is: python test_*.py
success_criteria: Script is standalone (no external config files)
Can be run immediately without setup

eval_questions.question: The generated script attempts to connect to controller at localhost:8443 but connection fails (refused). How does the script handle this error?
expected_outputs: Script catches connection error (ConnectionError, TimeoutError, or similar)
Script provides meaningful error message (not silent failure)
Script exits with non-zero status on connection failure
Cleanup code runs even on connection failure (finally block)
success_criteria: Error message is actionable (e.g., 'Check if controller is running')

eval_questions.question: Script must implement BGP session setup and verify neighbor state transitions (Idle → Connecting → Established). Verify the script includes state polling and timeout handling.
expected_outputs: Script polls BGP neighbor state in a loop
Timeout is set (e.g., 60 seconds for convergence)
Script breaks on BGP Established state
Script warns or fails if timeout exceeded
BGP state is logged at each iteration
success_criteria: Script does not assume protocol is up

eval_questions.question: After BGP converges, script starts traffic (1000 pps, 512-byte packets). Script must collect metrics and assert no-loss (rx_frames == tx_frames). Verify assertion logic.
expected_outputs: Traffic is started after protocol convergence
Script waits for traffic to settle (e.g., 10 seconds)
Metrics are collected (rx_frames, tx_frames, frame_loss)
Assertion: loss == 0 (or loss < threshold)
If assertion fails, error message includes actual metrics
success_criteria: Failed assertion shows actual vs expected
Script does not continue if assertion fails

eval_questions.question: Script must clean up gracefully: stop traffic, release ports, close connections, and generate a report (success/failure, metrics, duration). Verify cleanup runs even on error.
expected_outputs: Traffic is stopped (transmit state = stopped)
Connections are closed
Cleanup runs in finally block (executes even on error)
Report includes: test duration, pass/fail, metrics summary
Report is JSON or structured format
success_criteria: finally block executes on all code paths
No dangling connections after script exits
cleanup code does not itself raise exceptions
